This is basically a typical MMO but with an Action-RPG style combat rather than the TAB-Target system.
It is really important to play this with friends or be able to make friends easily through guilds. Takes a lot of time to fully understand all of the mechanics in the game and the main story quest is absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, but the game can be an awesome MMO when you get into it.

P2W is quite ok, as long you don't want to do any P2W you can still enjoy the game, there is a lot of content. But for me the RNG system is literally a PA joke... If you are not "carried" you might ending spending 10k+ hours more than your friends, and still less gear than them... Overall as RNG system, making huge difference between players I will not recommend the game. But if you are just somebody not competitive, and you want to got for treasures and content, yes is a nice game.

This is the best combat in any MMO ever made, however This is not an MMO, This is an ARPG with MMO aspects. The game loop reminds me way more of playing diablo than it does of playing warcraft, if that makes sense. You can, and often will, play this game solo for the majority of the grind to get geared. Much like going to the gym, its a personal effort and everyone is at a different stage of power. The end game is supposed to be PVP but in reality you end up wanting to do life skills, and it seems the devs realize that and are catering more towards that crowd... as the PVP side of the game is serviceable but has some flaws and room for improvement
This is a great game. But it's such as MMO grind that I just wouldn't recommend it to anyone who doesn't thinking putting their nuts in a vice is a good time
Still hands down has the best action combat of any game on the market.

It is the best MMO out there and with a very big distance by the second one...PVP, graphics and its enormous world make it a masterpiece..it has also a very good story but  many people out there just skip it because they are in rush...by few words enjoy the adventure that this game gives you
If all you care about is combat, this is the MMO for you, in my opinion it's the only strong feature of the game and the system of character locking classes should have been retired forever ago
Lots of people with 10k hours saying the game is bad. Always baffles me. How retarded do you have to be to spend 10k+ hours of your life and NOT recommend the game? Are you actually stupid?????

Just dont...... dont let the hours fool you. This is an alt account... my main has over 10k and honestly id say i regret most of them. I came back for the fist time in 5 years because of the 10 year anniversary rewards.. and I can say... God help the people still stuck in "Sunk Cost Fallacy - The Game"

I'm quitting this game because it requires age verification, even though I've been a Steam user for over 15 years, and they already have all my information – age, credit card details, and everything else. I'm not going to register with a company I don't know just to justify my age because of some stupid law from these ridiculous government officials. I'm the one who's going to lose out... If the company wants to see my money... REMOVE this ridiculous verification... I'm the one who should control what my children can and can't see on the internet, not a politician.

just dont play this garbage.
i started with crimson desert then tried black desert and i love it so much the characters you can make the online community the adventures beautiful game with so much adventure
Casual player, come back to it every so often, its fun for what it is.

I will start this with "yes, I have over 100 hours in this game and I know that looks like I am enjoying it." Which I am, but not for the reason you are thinking. I enjoy this game since it is aesthetically pleasing and I love the pets that you can get in the game. However, the trading between players directly sucks and it is more of a solo MMO with other players who also happen to be online at the same time you are. The fishing is nice since it allows you, the player, to leave the game running and for AFK material gathering to happen still... if you have the workers and the nodes for it. However, it all costs so much silver in the game and the decorations and costumes that you will see on other players cost IRL money. Yes, this is definitely a P2W game and yes, I have spent quite a bit on this game. 
I will actually go back to this game again soon, but at the moment, it has upset me a bit too much to stay online for the day.

I only stopped playing because I was forced onto a Turkish server where almost everyone spoke only Turkish, a language I have absolutely no understanding of.

MMORPGs are all about teaming up with other players and having social interactions, but this game effectively prevented me from doing that. I contacted support multiple times and begged them to let me switch regions. I even suggested deleting my account and starting over if that was what it took to end up on an English-speaking server. They refused to help in any way.

I genuinely liked the graphics, combat, and overall game mechanics. If not for this issue, I would probably have put thousands of hours into the game. It's unfortunate that such a great game was ruined for me by a problem that could have been solved with a simple region transfer.
pretty fun game! always something to do and all the different things you can do.

It's a beautiful game, and has all the trappings and grind and work and crafting of any MMO, it basically is a giant combining of like 200 MMOs

The Lore is what i like to call, Milk Toast

But it works
Such a fun game! Challenging and helps with patience, better with friends in my opinion. Growth in the process/progress of growth

So far enjoying the game always played wow just tired of the tab target liking the action combat of this though I here it’s grindy but I’m looking forward to the challenge and not just being given gear and I like how there’s different mounts flying and ships thats pretty cool graphics are nice to looking forward to getting a ship and working on fishing just hit lvl 57 haven’t played but for the past 3 days will add more positive or negative reviews after a month or so if your looking for something different from wow gw2 and ff14 this one might be the game

overall i was enjoying this game, right up until i found myself stuck in a couple of spots where it has been impossible tyo get out of, quests that got me lost with no way to get back where i was before, falling off cliffs when the direction pointer literally sent me into an ocean and other bizarre events. haven't played in over a year now because it is not possible to resume because i am just stuck in the endless loop of being directed to a very nearby place for my next quest target that is not nearby but apparently across an ocean, hiring a sailor and ship to get there resulted in nothing, and directions that repeatedly send me over a cliff, killing my horse. losing all my silver resurrecting my horse has become maddening so i just find it hard to feel compelled to try this again :(

pretty sad stuff because it is my first ow game and i was actually starting to get the hang of playing when all this happened. too many buggy weird things took place making too many quests impossible to complete. 

i am sure much of this my issues are due to my own inexperience with this sort of game, but when the directions literally send me on a journey to nowhere, it starts losing its sheen. 
when 'follow the owl' results in literally no bird appearing to follow, i start asking questions.
when the direction pointer tells me the barn is 10 points this-a-ways and literally never appears only to have the pointer tell me to cross an ocean that cannot be crossed because hiring sailors and a ship doesn't do anything, i start losing patience.
explained this issue to the developer and never did get a useful answer.
i suppose i can start over again but then i lose everything i gathered over the many months of prior playtime with it, so that doesn't appeal to me.
perhaps an experienced gamer might know how to get around this stuff but i found it mostly pretty intuitive until the bugginess showed up.

I selected this game as it looked fun, was a multiplayer game, and had a pretty neato player customization thingie, which all appealed to me. expecting to be able to make friends and play with people together online regularly but there doesn't seem to be a way to do that at all, so either i misunderstood or just never found this capability anywhere in the game. I could 'see' other players in the game, but found it impossible to interact with them and join anybody for any quests, so it really lost a lot for me rather quickly.
:(

Had no clue what i was doing at the beggining and 70 hours in i still dont know what im doing.

10/10 game.
♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ devs, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ players. wouldn't recommend to anyone for any reason at all

have life activity sims, and so many freebies, p2w need hard to invest to strong enough, so f2p / low spender still can enjoy
Good game for mindless mob slaying and the combat is really good

I really like the game and the tons of quests that it has.  Just don't get caught in the see without a way out it may take some time getting out LOL.  I would recommend the game to others its fun better with friends for sure.
If you want to hate gambling and quit going to the casino, you should play this game.

Great game, loving it so far, but the whole "you can't do pve if it's certain times on certain days [node wars, conquests]" is REALLY dumb.

Also, I initially hated it, but the game has a dating system that only like 15 people can use, because NPCs will only date ONE PLAYER at a time, and I have come to the conclusion that doing that in a g**ner game like this is one of the funniest ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ things I've seen in gaming.
Game is trash, they promise improvements, and then take 3 years to implement the smallest updates to the game, but will roll out new outfit in premium store each 2-3 months.

Tl;dr: Fantastic character creation, great visuals (including combat visuals), but bad MTX, lots of (Supposed) FOMO and RNG. Oh, and forced open world PvP.

BDO (For me) is an interesting game, because while it absolutely has some fantastic combat (Based on my experience with Corsair and Deadeye) and great visuals, alongside a very in-depth character creator. Despite this (And the fact you need to pay to even play the game), it feels very much like a F2P MMORPG, even including what feels like mobile game elements. 

I'm not gonna go too in depth on the combat and visuals, as both vary based on the character you play and how strong your rig is. I also won't go into the Lifeskill stuff as I never really felt motivated to REALLY try that stuff before making this review (In part to the negatives listed below) 

As for the character creator, my only real gripe with it was the fact some hairstyles were exclusive to certain classes, meaning I can't have my Deadeye share the same hairstyle as my Corsair, especially when I intended to have them be the same person. My other issue with it is that altering the characters base height at all will cause them to constantly default to a normal height during cutscenes (And its visible as well)

The story of the game is... its alright. First 3 acts of it (Balenos, Serendia, and Calpheon) are neat, main quests are all fully voiced (With some pretty good VA work, though the lip-syncing and cutscenes are a little jarring at times), but after the Calpheon section, it just becomes a reading simulator. Deadeyes Awakening quest did have VA work, but thats sorta out of place in the story, as she was added somewhat recently (I think a year ago, can't recall), but apart from that, all of the main story content up to Drieghan (So Mediah, Valenica, and Kamasylvia) have no cutscenes or VA work. If you want a good story, BDO does not have it after Calpheon, I'm afraid.

As for the negatives, I'm going to first go over the RNG. Gonna come out of the gate here and say that if you do not like the RNG in games like Destiny, Warframe (To an extent), or whatever other game you come from. BELIEVE ME, BDO has it REALLY bad. There's RNG for gaining knowledge on enemies (which is somewhat critical, as you need at least the most basic grade of knowledge to see their health bar go down, and most knowledge drop chances are 2%, maybe a little more. Yes, you can see their health bar slightly change color if you don't have the knowledge, but its still silly. It doesn't help that some lifeskill stuff requires energy, and the main way you increase your max energy is by gaining knowledge on everything in the game. And a good lot of that is through enemies), RNG for upgrading gear (Which can fail, causing you to lose max durability, revert your gear to a lower level, or outright DELETE your item if you don't use protection items), among more RNG I may not even know of, cause I never got far enough to deal with it.

My next big negative is the MTX (And also the supposed FOMO). Its... its really bad, especially for a game that has a buy-in price. Most outfit boxes require at least 2,000 Pearls (Which is basically $20 USD), and accessories can cost anywhere between 300-700 Pearls, based on what I saw. There's also 3 different "subscription" bundles that do various things and can be bought in 30-day or 90-day increments (They do giveaway free ones sometimes, but nowhere near as long as the ones in the store) Some of the outfits are... alright, I guess. Its normally just a case of "More money = more sexy" which is just a whatever thing. If you like it, cool. From what I have also hear, if cosmetics leave the shop, they almost never return, which creates the obvious FOMO dilemma in other games.

My main final negative is actually the biggest reason why I've dropped the game. Forced Open World PvP. In a game with incredibly punishing RNG, that issue can only be exasperated by the fact you can have an attention-starved loser come up to you while you're grinding (or lifeskill grinding), and be able to kill you (Yes, both parties need to accept the war declaration and stuff like that, but its still ♥♥♥♥♥♥. And apparently, it was worse before). Put simple, that sucks. Especially when this can cause your Crystals (Another source of power that requires grinding), to shatter and become unusable. There is apparently a tactic non-PvPers will use where they will revive, then let themselves die to mobs causing the initial aggressor to lose their crystals. Some will say thats bad, but I think its a great deterrent to stop people from just randomly attacking others. That said, there is already a dedicated PvP mode. Just get rid of the Open World PvP, and it'd be great. 

I should note that I did somewhat enjoy my time with BDO, mainly due to the great combat the 2 classes I played had. But with all the aforementioned negatives, I just can't recommend it, and I don't know if this will change, what with Pearl Abyss focusing a lot of time into Crimson Desert (Which is completely reasonable)

This game forces a tutorial, does not let you change your button configuration during the tutorial, the tutorial NPC's face got stuck on my screen so I couldn't even see half of what was going on. After all these years they still haven't figured out how to make the game user friendly? Do not recommend.

WELP come back after years of not playing ... just to try it out again .... ha .... ha ..... nope .... your account was deleted 5 (idk) years ago cuz ya missed an email and there aint ♥♥♥♥ you can do about it or that they will try to do about it ... ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ your new game was good .... now im done with both.

As a fresh player on PC, (I used to play on console) The P2W aspects are very tame. To put in perspective, I have about, 1-3 entire months of the ingame packs recieved for free through various events and codes. I have been lucky enough to not have to buy one of the packs by itself at all this entire 300 hours and I will be free of having to buy one untill likely september.  It being May right now, if you manage to play for 4 months and you can not scrounge together $20 dollars for a few perks that make the game experience better for yourself, due to being against "P2W" remember that every single player has the exact same opportunity to buy any of these things. So in reality, its you just holding yourself back. o7
really good game, they reworked the gearing too so now its really good to get started with good gear on a seasonal server, well done, pvp is fun too

i like how they keep streamlining early game progress (especially in seasons) for newer or returning players, but the lack of meaningful end-game PVE content means that once you hit good gear, there isn't much content besides doing routes for silver farming :(

If you're new and want to get started in this game, do it with your friends—that's the only way it'll be fun. If you want to start it solo... well, I hope your sanity holds up through all those hours of grinding.

Game is very clunky. menus were too elaborate or non informative. movement was okay but got stuck in animations alot. had no patience for that level of annoyance as a new player. after closing the game my PC started doing random stuff like everything i clicked was a hyperlink. not mad for ten bucks but its not new player friendly and too much ♥♥♥♥ is being presented out of the gate. the devs should trim it back and remove the store and event ads and everything until you hit end game. just too overwhelming which is sad because the game looks really cool and im sure the community would like more players and im sure there are elitist in the community ready to tell me im wrong that's okay this is my opinion but spending three hours fighting with movement controls is enough and im pissed that my PC's normal functions were effected as well. My PC is really good too.

Great combat action and character development

Don't start, pay or play this game.

The gear enhancement system prevents a fair upgrade ... because it is - and becomes increasingly difficult, tyrannical, hysterical AND costly!

It all boils down to gear score, which demands increasingly higher AP (attack points) and DP (defense points).

To get anywhere, or to progress to significant grind spots after level 60 - not to mention end game content - you need either to turn into a hardcore grinder, need to spend a LOT of time and/or real money.

It feels like accountants dictate the development.

If you continue being a casual player or grinder - the game is fair enough.
as a casual player such as myself, i can say its alright

Its never ending grind but satisfying combat i got further on Xbox sadly

I tried gw2, osrs, poe 1&2, sro(goat) in this year to ease my mmo rpg hunger but none of them really satisfied me. Bdo is one of the best (if not best) modern mmo rpg in the market. Of course it has many flaws just like the other mmos but it still manages to be a stabile and good game. 

It isnt "really" p2w, in fact its pay to loose in most cases since gear upgrade system entirely relies on RNG even you bought the most expensive upgrade items. Like you can spend hundreds of dollars and gain absolute nothing, purely rng.

The only things are considered as p2w(more like pay to grind easier) are Tents, (they are like portable npc, you can buy potions, repair gear while grinding) pets, (pets loot for you automatically which in this case they are the most p2w element) and maids (they let you to access your storage remotely with a 20min cooldown). Only the pets make your life way easier but game already gives you several pets, which are mostly t1, they collect loot every 4 second. not really bad but honestly i cant call those things p2w. Most grind spots are already near a town, so u dont need maid or tent. you can have several t1 pet, lets say you have 5 of them, they collect 5 items every 4 seconds which isnt bad. 

so yeah overall a nice game, just doesnt really respects your time since gear grind is always competitive unlike gw2-like games, but the competition makes the game more fun. 

Most importantly game has the best classes, best skills and the best animations among the other mmos undeniably. There are at least 2x more combos than mortal kombat... Combos can connect within themselves.. so you just dont memorize the combo inputs, you need to memorize which skill can lengthen  the current combo etc..
never played an mmo but it was fun for a while, might go back if im real bored

Decided to give this game a chance, forgot I had made a character a few years back and wanted to start fresh... it makes you wait for a character to be deleted... Flexible character customization. Not many male characters to pick from. Fantastic graphics. Good storyline. Isobelle is definitely better than Jerette, honestly. Also, I love that you level up pretty fast in the game.

I don't know if there is competition between Live Service games on who is going to have the worst possible Servers, but I took a break from PoE ( Path of Exile ) because servers are simply not working... apparently for the competition of who's servers are the worst, my votes goes to PA and BDO, how can such big game have servers that are at random disconnecting and reconnecting over and over again?

And that's not all, if You got lucky and ur game close while server had mental breakdown... You may endup with locked launcher... go figure out how to unlock it...

And yeah, the series of Magnus quests are there the check how stupid You are apparently, how far can You mentally damage Yourself, I personally left these quests because it is lack of respect towards it's players to provide something like that...

I have spent about 100 USD on that game in 2017 when, then they deleted my account cuz I was not playing when they were making migration, now I returned and well... I need to say I wasted about 50 USD on them, and that's the last money they are getting.

Servers are barely working, they are a lot worse than in PoE because in PoE You just have occasional lags like Your ping gets to ~2000 or so ( my record and I have a picture of it was reaching over 45.000 ping ) 
In case of BDO? the game just gets You GTFO Ticket and You can waste about 5 minutes of Your life trying to get back to the game... 

Or about 30 minutes if it just kicks You out and then locks Your Launcher...

Just to show that I'm serious about the PoE, here is my 47.000 ping: ( PNG Picture on Google drive )
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EHe8XkRYdkGBXFZ7omSJczcEIKmvwTwR/view?usp=sharing

And yes, BDO servers are much worse than that, like I said, PoE at least dont kick u out...
I have already lost world Bosses and nearly died losing XP because game just kicked me out but apparently servers allowed mobs to keep hitting me while I was not even in the game?

...
regional entitlement makes this game unplayable for me since I originally registered in SA server and now I want to play on SEA server because I moved, thanks ...
